{{nihongo|'''Suwa Shrine'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suwa Shrine'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suwa Shrine'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suwa Shrine'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suwa Shrine'''|諏訪神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

[[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)|version=107339877|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 諏訪神社 |画像 = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[出雲市]]別所町72 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 建御名方神、八束水臣津野命{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}} |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]論社、旧[[村社]]{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}} |創建 = 不明（天正12年（1584年）{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}}？）{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}} |本殿 = [[春日造]]{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}}あるいは[[大社造]]変態{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}} |別名 = 白瀧権現{{sfn|島根県神社庁}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月9日{{sfn|島根県神社庁}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]別所町に所在する[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|諏訪神社の境内|250px]] 創立年代は不明だが、[[天正]]12年建立の[[棟札]]がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載「出雲社」および『[[延喜式神名帳]]』所載「[[出雲神社 (延喜式)|出雲神社]]」に比定する説があるが{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}、出雲社・出雲神社の論社は複数あり諏訪神社説に対して批判も強い{{sfn|石塚|1986|pp=156-189}}。 [[明治]]5年（1872年）、村社{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}}。 == 祭神 == '''[[建御名方神]]''' :18世紀に編纂された地誌『[[雲陽誌]]』によると、宮山に高さ40間あまりの岩壁があって、傍に高さ30間・周囲5丈ばかりの丸い立岩があり「帆柱石」あるいは「{{ルビ|檣|かち|rp1=［|rp2=］}}石」と呼ばれている{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}{{efn|帆柱石は現存しない{{sfn|島根県神社庁}}。}}。諏訪神社の近隣に所在する[[鰐淵寺]]を開山した智春上人が[[信濃国]]から来訪した際に3人の老翁が船で出迎えたのち、船具を分けて3所に飛び去ったという伝承がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。そのうちの1人が諏訪明神であったのだが、彼が取った船具が帆柱で、それが帆柱石であるとされる{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。 '''[[八束水臣津野命]]''' :『雲陽誌』には「建御名方命なり」とあって八束水臣津野命は記載されていない{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=271}}。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|20em}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|author=石塚尊俊|title=古代出雲の研究：神と神を祀るものの消長|year=1986|publisher=佼成出版社|ref={{sfnref|石塚|1986}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=蘆田伊人|title=雲陽誌|series=大日本地誌大系27|date=1930年|origdate=1717|publisher=雄山閣|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|蘆田編|1930}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=高橋忠|chapter=124 出雲神社|editor=式内社研究会|title=式内社調査報告20 山陰道3|year=1983|pages=512-515|ref={{sfnref|高橋|1983}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor1=高橋忠|editor2=水師重吉|editor3=伊藤征男|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) 諏訪神社|title=神国島根（復刻版）|page=253|publisher=島根県神社庁|ref={{sfnref|高橋他編|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=諏訪神社 - 神社詳細|publisher=島根県神社庁|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|島根県神社庁}}}} {{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] ==End Japanese==

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]別所町に所在する[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|諏訪神社の境内|250px]] 創立年代は不明だが、[[天正]]12年建立の[[棟札]]がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載「出雲社」および『[[延喜式神名帳]]』所載「[[出雲神社 (延喜式)|出雲神社]]」に比定する説があるが{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}、出雲社・出雲神社の論社は複数あり諏訪神社説に対して批判も強い{{sfn|石塚|1986|pp=156-189}}。 [[明治]]5年（1872年）、村社{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}}。 == 祭神 == '''[[建御名方神]]''' :18世紀に編纂された地誌『[[雲陽誌]]』によると、宮山に高さ40間あまりの岩壁があって、傍に高さ30間・周囲5丈ばかりの丸い立岩があり「帆柱石」あるいは「{{ルビ|檣|かち|rp1=［|rp2=］}}石」と呼ばれている{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}{{efn|帆柱石は現存しない{{sfn|島根県神社庁}}。}}。諏訪神社の近隣に所在する[[鰐淵寺]]を開山した智春上人が[[信濃国]]から来訪した際に3人の老翁が船で出迎えたのち、船具を分けて3所に飛び去ったという伝承がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。そのうちの1人が諏訪明神であったのだが、彼が取った船具が帆柱で、それが帆柱石であるとされる{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。 '''[[八束水臣津野命]]''' :『雲陽誌』には「建御名方命なり」とあって八束水臣津野命は記載されていない{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=271}}。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|20em}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|author=石塚尊俊|title=古代出雲の研究：神と神を祀るものの消長|year=1986|publisher=佼成出版社|ref={{sfnref|石塚|1986}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=蘆田伊人|title=雲陽誌|series=大日本地誌大系27|date=1930年|origdate=1717|publisher=雄山閣|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|蘆田編|1930}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=高橋忠|chapter=124 出雲神社|editor=式内社研究会|title=式内社調査報告20 山陰道3|year=1983|pages=512-515|ref={{sfnref|高橋|1983}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor1=高橋忠|editor2=水師重吉|editor3=伊藤征男|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) 諏訪神社|title=神国島根（復刻版）|page=253|publisher=島根県神社庁|ref={{sfnref|高橋他編|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=諏訪神社 - 神社詳細|publisher=島根県神社庁|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|島根県神社庁}}}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] ==End Japanese==

[[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|諏訪神社の境内|250px]] 創立年代は不明だが、[[天正]]12年建立の[[棟札]]がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載「出雲社」および『[[延喜式神名帳]]』所載「[[出雲神社 (延喜式)|出雲神社]]」に比定する説があるが{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}、出雲社・出雲神社の論社は複数あり諏訪神社説に対して批判も強い{{sfn|石塚|1986|pp=156-189}}。 [[明治]]5年（1872年）、村社{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}}。 == 祭神 == '''[[建御名方神]]''' :18世紀に編纂された地誌『[[雲陽誌]]』によると、宮山に高さ40間あまりの岩壁があって、傍に高さ30間・周囲5丈ばかりの丸い立岩があり「帆柱石」あるいは「{{ルビ|檣|かち|rp1=［|rp2=］}}石」と呼ばれている{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}{{efn|帆柱石は現存しない{{sfn|島根県神社庁}}。}}。諏訪神社の近隣に所在する[[鰐淵寺]]を開山した智春上人が[[信濃国]]から来訪した際に3人の老翁が船で出迎えたのち、船具を分けて3所に飛び去ったという伝承がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。そのうちの1人が諏訪明神であったのだが、彼が取った船具が帆柱で、それが帆柱石であるとされる{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。 '''[[八束水臣津野命]]''' :『雲陽誌』には「建御名方命なり」とあって八束水臣津野命は記載されていない{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=271}}。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|20em}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|author=石塚尊俊|title=古代出雲の研究：神と神を祀るものの消長|year=1986|publisher=佼成出版社|ref={{sfnref|石塚|1986}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=蘆田伊人|title=雲陽誌|series=大日本地誌大系27|date=1930年|origdate=1717|publisher=雄山閣|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|蘆田編|1930}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=高橋忠|chapter=124 出雲神社|editor=式内社研究会|title=式内社調査報告20 山陰道3|year=1983|pages=512-515|ref={{sfnref|高橋|1983}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor1=高橋忠|editor2=水師重吉|editor3=伊藤征男|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) 諏訪神社|title=神国島根（復刻版）|page=253|publisher=島根県神社庁|ref={{sfnref|高橋他編|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=諏訪神社 - 神社詳細|publisher=島根県神社庁|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|島根県神社庁}}}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] ==End Japanese==

[[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|諏訪神社の境内|250px]] 創立年代は不明だが、[[天正]]12年建立の[[棟札]]がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載「出雲社」および『[[延喜式神名帳]]』所載「[[出雲神社 (延喜式)|出雲神社]]」に比定する説があるが{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}、出雲社・出雲神社の論社は複数あり諏訪神社説に対して批判も強い{{sfn|石塚|1986|pp=156-189}}。 [[明治]]5年（1872年）、村社{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}}。 == 祭神 == '''[[建御名方神]]''' :18世紀に編纂された地誌『[[雲陽誌]]』によると、宮山に高さ40間あまりの岩壁があって、傍に高さ30間・周囲5丈ばかりの丸い立岩があり「帆柱石」あるいは「{{ルビ|檣|かち|rp1=［|rp2=］}}石」と呼ばれている{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}{{efn|帆柱石は現存しない{{sfn|島根県神社庁}}。}}。諏訪神社の近隣に所在する[[鰐淵寺]]を開山した智春上人が[[信濃国]]から来訪した際に3人の老翁が船で出迎えたのち、船具を分けて3所に飛び去ったという伝承がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。そのうちの1人が諏訪明神であったのだが、彼が取った船具が帆柱で、それが帆柱石であるとされる{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。 '''[[八束水臣津野命]]''' :『雲陽誌』には「建御名方命なり」とあって八束水臣津野命は記載されていない{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=271}}。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|20em}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|author=石塚尊俊|title=古代出雲の研究：神と神を祀るものの消長|year=1986|publisher=佼成出版社|ref={{sfnref|石塚|1986}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=蘆田伊人|title=雲陽誌|series=大日本地誌大系27|date=1930年|origdate=1717|publisher=雄山閣|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|蘆田編|1930}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=高橋忠|chapter=124 出雲神社|editor=式内社研究会|title=式内社調査報告20 山陰道3|year=1983|pages=512-515|ref={{sfnref|高橋|1983}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor1=高橋忠|editor2=水師重吉|editor3=伊藤征男|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) 諏訪神社|title=神国島根（復刻版）|page=253|publisher=島根県神社庁|ref={{sfnref|高橋他編|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=諏訪神社 - 神社詳細|publisher=島根県神社庁|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|島根県神社庁}}}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は{{jalink|島根県|Q132751}}{{jalink|出雲市|Q825166}}別所町に所在する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 == 概要 == [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|諏訪神社の境内|250px]] 創立年代は不明だが、{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}12年建立の{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。『{{jalink|出雲国風土記|Q11395858}}』所載「出雲社」および『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』所載「{{jalink|出雲神社 (延喜式)||出雲神社}}」に比定する説があるが{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}、出雲社・出雲神社の論社は複数あり諏訪神社説に対して批判も強い{{sfn|石塚|1986|pp=156-189}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（1872年）、村社{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}}。 == 祭神 == '''{{jalink|建御名方神}}''' :18世紀に編纂された地誌『{{jalink|雲陽誌}}』によると、宮山に高さ40間あまりの岩壁があって、傍に高さ30間・周囲5丈ばかりの丸い立岩があり「帆柱石」あるいは「{{ルビ|檣|かち|rp1=［|rp2=］}}石」と呼ばれている{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}{{efn|帆柱石は現存しない{{sfn|島根県神社庁}}。}}。諏訪神社の近隣に所在する{{jalink|鰐淵寺|Q11674322}}を開山した智春上人が{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}から来訪した際に3人の老翁が船で出迎えたのち、船具を分けて3所に飛び去ったという伝承がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。そのうちの1人が諏訪明神であったのだが、彼が取った船具が帆柱で、それが帆柱石であるとされる{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。 '''{{jalink|八束水臣津野命}}''' :『雲陽誌』には「建御名方命なり」とあって八束水臣津野命は記載されていない{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=271}}。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|20em}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|author=石塚尊俊|title=古代出雲の研究：神と神を祀るものの消長|year=1986|publisher=佼成出版社|ref={{sfnref|石塚|1986}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=蘆田伊人|title=雲陽誌|series=大日本地誌大系27|date=1930年|origdate=1717|publisher=雄山閣|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|蘆田編|1930}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=高橋忠|chapter=124 出雲神社|editor=式内社研究会|title=式内社調査報告20 山陰道3|year=1983|pages=512-515|ref={{sfnref|高橋|1983}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor1=高橋忠|editor2=水師重吉|editor3=伊藤征男|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) 諏訪神社|title=神国島根（復刻版）|page=253|publisher=島根県神社庁|ref={{sfnref|高橋他編|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=諏訪神社 - 神社詳細|publisher=島根県神社庁|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|島根県神社庁}}}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 諏訪神社 |画像 = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|島根県|Q132751}}{{jalink|出雲市|Q825166}}別所町72 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 建御名方神、八束水臣津野命{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}} |創建 = 不明（天正12年（1584年）{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}}？）{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}} |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}{{sfn|高橋他編|1996|p=253}}あるいは{{jalink|大社造|Q387874}}変態{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}} |別名 = 白瀧権現{{sfn|島根県神社庁}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月9日{{sfn|島根県神社庁}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は{{jalink|島根県|Q132751}}{{jalink|出雲市|Q825166}}別所町に所在する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|諏訪神社の境内|250px]] 創立年代は不明だが、{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}12年建立の{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。『{{jalink|出雲国風土記|Q11395858}}』所載「出雲社」および『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』所載「{{jalink|出雲神社 (延喜式)||出雲神社}}」に比定する説があるが{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}、出雲社・出雲神社の論社は複数あり諏訪神社説に対して批判も強い{{sfn|石塚|1986|pp=156-189}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（1872年）、村社{{sfn|高橋|1983|p=515}}。 === 祭神 === '''{{jalink|建御名方神}}''' :18世紀に編纂された地誌『{{jalink|雲陽誌}}』によると、宮山に高さ40間あまりの岩壁があって、傍に高さ30間・周囲5丈ばかりの丸い立岩があり「帆柱石」あるいは「{{ルビ|檣|かち|rp1=［|rp2=］}}石」と呼ばれている{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}{{efn|帆柱石は現存しない{{sfn|島根県神社庁}}。}}。諏訪神社の近隣に所在する{{jalink|鰐淵寺|Q11674322}}を開山した智春上人が{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}から来訪した際に3人の老翁が船で出迎えたのち、船具を分けて3所に飛び去ったという伝承がある{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。そのうちの1人が諏訪明神であったのだが、彼が取った船具が帆柱で、それが帆柱石であるとされる{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=272}}。 '''{{jalink|八束水臣津野命}}''' :『雲陽誌』には「建御名方命なり」とあって八束水臣津野命は記載されていない{{sfn|蘆田編|1930|p=271}}。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|20em}} === 参考文献 === * {{cite book|和書|author=石塚尊俊|title=古代出雲の研究：神と神を祀るものの消長|year=1986|publisher=佼成出版社|ref={{sfnref|石塚|1986}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=蘆田伊人|title=雲陽誌|series=大日本地誌大系27|date=1930年|origdate=1717|publisher=雄山閣|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|蘆田編|1930}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=高橋忠|chapter=124 出雲神社|editor=式内社研究会|title=式内社調査報告20 山陰道3|year=1983|pages=512-515|ref={{sfnref|高橋|1983}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor1=高橋忠|editor2=水師重吉|editor3=伊藤征男|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) 諏訪神社|title=神国島根（復刻版）|page=253|publisher=島根県神社庁|ref={{sfnref|高橋他編|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=諏訪神社 - 神社詳細|publisher=島根県神社庁|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|島根県神社庁}}}} {{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや}} == Categories == Torii |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Bessho-chō, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{jalink|munafuda|Q8008834}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}} and the “{{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)||Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{jalink|Unyōshi}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{jalink|Gakuen-ji|Q11674322}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{jalink|Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto}}''' :The {{jalink|Unyōshi}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}} and the “{{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)||Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{jalink|Unyōshi}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{jalink|Gakuen-ji|Q11674322}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{jalink|Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto}}''' :The {{jalink|Unyōshi}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{jalink|Gakuen-ji|Q11674322}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{jalink|Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto}}''' :The {{jalink|Unyōshi}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Bessho-chō, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{jalink|munafuda|Q8008834}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}} and the “{{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)||Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{jalink|Unyōshi}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{jalink|Gakuen-ji|Q11674322}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{jalink|Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto}}''' :The {{jalink|Unyōshi}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Bessho-chō, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{jalink|munafuda|Q8008834}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}} and the “{{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)||Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{jalink|Unyōshi}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{jalink|Gakuen-ji|Q11674322}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{jalink|Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto}}''' :The {{jalink|Unyōshi}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Bessho-chō, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{jalink|munafuda|Q8008834}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}} and the “{{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)||Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{jalink|Unyōshi}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{jalink|Gakuen-ji|Q11674322}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{jalink|Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto}}''' :The {{jalink|Unyōshi}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Bessho-chō, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{jalink|munafuda|Q8008834}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}} and the “{{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)||Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{jalink|Unyōshi}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{jalink|Gakuen-ji|Q11674322}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{jalink|Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto}}''' :The {{jalink|Unyōshi}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

== said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories ==
