(出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono Shrine|lt=Itsumono Shrine|WD=Q135041043|1=Itsumono Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市別所町72', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:諏訪神社 (出雲市別所町)]] {{wikidata link|Q135070129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} 72 Bessho-chō |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Takeminakata-no-Kami, Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), formerly {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} |Founded = Unknown (possibly Tenshō 12 (1584){{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}}?){{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996|p=253}} or a variant of {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}} |Alternate name = Shirataki Gongen{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 9{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Bessho-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Bessho Suwa Jinja Keidai.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Suwa Shrine|250px]] The date of establishment is unknown, but there exists a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridgepole inscription) dating from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. There is a theory identifying this shrine with the “Izumo no Yashiro” recorded in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}} and the “{{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Engishiki)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}, but there are multiple candidate shrines for Izumo no Yashiro / Izumo Shrine, and criticism of the Suwa Shrine theory is strong{{sfn|Ishizuka|1986|pp=156–189}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was designated a village shrine{{sfn|Takahashi|1983|p=515}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}''' :According to the 18th-century topographical work {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, there was a rock cliff more than 40 ken high on Mount Miya, and beside it a round standing rock about 30 ken high and roughly 5 jō in circumference, called “Hobashira-ishi” or “{{ruby|檣|kachi|rp1=[|rp2=]}}-ishi”{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}{{efn|The Hobashira-ishi no longer exists{{sfn|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}.}}. There is a tradition that when the monk Chishun, who founded {{ill|lt=Gakuen-ji|Q11674322|WD=Q11674322|1=Gakuen-ji}} located near Suwa Shrine, came from {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, three old men welcomed him by boat and then divided the ship’s fittings and flew off to three places{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. One of them was Suwa Myōjin; the fitting he took was the mast, which is said to be the Hobashira-ishi{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=272}}. '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' :The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Unyōshi|1=UNKNOWN}} states “it is Takeminakata-no-Mikoto,” and does not list Yatsukamizuomitsuno-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Ashida, ed.|1930|p=271}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|title=Studies on Ancient Izumo: The Rise and Fall of the Gods and Those Who Worshipped Them|year=1986|publisher=Kōsei Publishing|ref={{sfnref|Ishizuka|1986}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Ashida Hito|title=Unyōshi|series=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 27|date=1930|origdate=1717|publisher=Yūzankaku|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/1194083|ref={{sfnref|Ashida, ed.|1930}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takahashi Tadashi|chapter=124 Izumo Shrine|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report 20: San’indō 3|year=1983|pages=512–515|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1983}}}} * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor1=Takahashi Tadashi|editor2=Mizushi Jūkichi|editor3=Itō Masao|date=1996|origdate=1981|chapter=(26) Suwa Shrine|title=Shinkoku Shimane (reprint edition)|page=253|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al., eds.|1996}}}} * {{cite web|title=Suwa Shrine – Shrine Details|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/6492e126c892fd9370947c084a6d5f91a3e097ff.html|accessdate=2025-08-31|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefectural Shrine Association}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]]
